blocks.content: The yard was quiet. Down the block, a car passed and was gone.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: That kiss was supposed to be for something.
She looked up.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The water tower one. It happened and I didn't feel what I thought I was supposed to feel. I just felt — caught off guard. Like it wasn't the moment. Like I'd been holding something for the right moment and this wasn't it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Is that — did you feel that way? With Ryan?
A pause. Honest.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The first time he kissed me. After homecoming freshman year, before we were even together. It was fine. He was kind about it. He always was. But after — I went home and I thought: that's not it. That wasn't it. And I didn't know what I was measuring it against.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I think you did know.
She was quiet for a long moment.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah. I think I did.
The porch light buzzed. He listened to it. She didn't move away from the railing.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The dance. Last year. When the song stopped.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I thought about that for a long time.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Me too.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Why didn't you say something after. That night. On AIM. You were right there.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Because I didn't know what to say. It was — I didn't know if I'd imagined it. The way you leaned in.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You didn't imagine it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I needed to hear you say that.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I didn't know how to say it either. I sat at my laptop for an hour.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Me too. I had a whole thing typed out. Deleted all of it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We're really bad at this.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The worst.
She laughed — not a big laugh, just a small exhale of one — and it broke something loose in the air between them.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: What are we doing, Kit.
Not quite a question. More like something she needed to hear out loud.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I don't know.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We're going to different states. In six months.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Six months. Yeah. I've been counting.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: California and Minnesota.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I know.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Stop saying I know.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What else do I say.
She didn't have an answer for that. Neither did he. They sat with it — the thing between them, finally named, still not solved. He could see her breath in the cold.
Then he said it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Come to prom with me.
She looked at him.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Not as the group. As my date. With me.
The porch light buzzed softly. She held his gaze for a long moment — the full look, checking if something was real.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah. Okay.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

He nodded. She looked back at the yard. They didn't move toward each other. They just stood on the porch in the February cold — the thing still unsolved, one more thing added to it, and somehow that was exactly enough.
